business.headline: HDFC Bank appoints ex-poll body chief Rajiv Kumar as chairman; RBI nod awaitedHDFC Bank has appointed former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as its new chairman, subject to RBI approval, replacing Atanu Chakraborty.
Yen sinks to 40-year low, dollar slips but stays near 13-month highThe Japanese yen weakened to its lowest levels against the US currency since 1986.

Govt to lift petrol and diesel sales curbs from July 1 as supply concerns easeThe precautionary measures were introduced to protect domestic fuel supplies at a time when uncertainty in the global energy market was increasing.

From ITR deadlines to passport fees: 5 financial changes from July 1 you must knowWhether you're planning to file your ITR, apply for a passport or simply keep track of your finances, July 1 brings a few important changes you shouldn't ignore. Here are five key financial updates that could matter to you. Which ones will affect you the most?

Markets snap winning streak as weak monsoon worries trigger profit bookingThe BSE Sensex fell 372.10 points, or 0.48%, to close at 76,728.37, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 109.75 points, or 0.46%, to settle at 23,946.25.

Amazon, Flipkart quick commerce push dents Blinkit and SwiggyAmazon and Flipkart are rapidly expanding their quick commerce networks across India. The push has deepened investor concerns over profitability, valuations and a prolonged price war.

EPFO 3.0: 5 PF mistakes that could delay your transfer when switching jobsChanging jobs comes with plenty of paperwork, and transferring your PF is one task you shouldn't ignore. While EPFO 3.0 promises a faster and smoother process, a few simple mistakes could still delay your money. Are you making any of them?

Gold price has crashed nearly Rs 50,000 from its peak. Will prices fall further?Gold has slipped sharply from its MCX record high as investors reassess the US rate outlook. The fall has given buyers some relief, but volatility remains tied to inflation, oil and Federal Reserve signals.

BAT to slash 9,000 jobs amid shift to smoke-free productsThe move is part of the company's wider plan to reduce costs while investing more in smoke-free products such as vaping devices and nicotine pouches.

Nearly Rs 9,000 crore in unclaimed insurance: How to check if any is yoursNearly Rs 8,974 crore in insurance money remains unclaimed with insurers across India. Old policies, missing nominee details and outdated records could leave rightful dues untouched for years.

Samsung, SK Hynix's $518 billion AI bet: Why the chip giants are expandingAs the AI boom fuels a global race for computing power, Samsung and SK Hynix are making one of their biggest expansion bets yet. Here's why the chip giants are investing $518 billion.

Home sales slip 6%: Why are buyers pulling back across top cities?ANAROCK said housing sales across the top seven cities fell in Q2 2026 even as new launches rose. The data showed a more balanced market as war-linked uncertainty and IT sector concerns weighed on buyers.

One missed EMI, endless harassment: The dark side of digital lending boomDigital lending has made borrowing faster and easier than ever before. Yet behind the promise of instant cash lies a troubling reality: for some borrowers, a single missed EMI can lead to harassment, intimidation and financial distress.

Think your Rs 5 lakh health insurance cover is enough? It may not beA new analysis of more than one lakh policyholders by insurance platform CoverSure has found that a majority of people above the age of 50 have health insurance coverage of less than Rs 5 lakh. Even more worrying, 17% of policyholders with chronic medical conditions also have a sum insured below Rs 5 lakh.

ITR filing 2026: July's biggest income tax deadlines explainedJuly brings six key income tax deadlines, including the 31 July ITR filing cut-off. Missing them can lead to late fees, interest and limits on carrying forward losses.

Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude stays low, FII mood improvesThe BSE Sensex was up 48.58 points, or 0.06%, at 77,149.05 at around 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 44.65 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,100.65 as of 9:23 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will markets rise or fall after a 3-day break?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,093 around 8:07 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open close to Friday's closing level of 24,056, suggesting a muted start to the trading session.

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

India reclaims 5th spot among world's biggest stock markets after Asian selloffIndia has regained the world's fifth-largest stock market position after Taiwan and South Korea slipped. The shift highlights how easing crude prices, foreign buying and regional corrections are reshaping rankings.

Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani to step down at year-endKotak Mahindra Bank has begun searching for a successor after CEO Ashok Vaswani said he will not seek reappointment when his term ends in December 2026. The move comes as the lender seeks faster growth after regulatory scrutiny and may keep investors focused on leadership continuity.

entertainment.headline: Angry Birds Movie 3 trailer sees Red juggle fatherhood and saving the world The trailer for The Angry Birds Movie 3 is finally here, but there's more than another battle with the pigs. Here's what the first look reveals about Red's next adventure.

Sunny Deol wanted to make Damini 2. Here's why it didn't happen Sunny Deol has revealed that he spent years trying to make Damini 2, but the sequel never took off. The actor explains how Ikka became the courtroom drama that finally brought him back to the genre.

Singer Jasmine Sandlas faces backlash over late Hyderabad show, lip-sync claims A concert attendee has accused Jasmine Sandlas of reaching her Hyderabad show more than three hours late and lip-syncing on stage. The viral post has drawn criticism from fans, while the singer and organisers have yet to respond.

What happened to Jasmin Bhasin? Actor reveals reason for hospitalisation Actor Jasmin Bhasin said she was hospitalised in Dubai after being diagnosed with terminal ileitis and a severe infection. She celebrated her birthday with boyfriend Aly Goni in the hospital.

Uorfi Javed denies claims of converting to Hinduism and changing her name Actor Uorfi Javed has rejected claims that she converted and changed her name. The exchange escalated after the actor said she received triggering messages and false remarks about her appearance.

Ikka trailer: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna in a new Ram-Raavan battle for justice The trailer of Ikka shows actor Sunny Deol's lawyer Arjun Mehra defending accused Shauryamann Gaur, played by actor Akshaye Khanna, in an attempted murder case. The film draws parallels to Ram-Raavan's battle for justice.

Kannada actor Krishi Thapanda breaks silence on man's death at her Bengaluru home Actor Krishi Thapanda has broken her silence after businessman Vaishak was found dead at her Bengaluru home. Here's what the actor said as the police investigation continues.

Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan faces backlash from Rajput group over title, first glimpse Ajay Devgn's film Chauhaan has drawn objections from the Kshatriya Parishad over its title and first glimpse. The group says Rajput history and identity should not be used for contemporary political narratives.

Kyunki's darkest callback yet: Marital rape, murder and history repeating itself Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi 2 revisits its most traumatic storyline as Nandini shoots Parth to save Vaishnavi. The sequence mirrors Tulsi's earlier sacrifice and brings the show's debate on consent and abuse back into focus.

Eetha: NCP demands Vithabai's name in title as Shraddha Kapoor's film sparks buzz Actor Shraddha Kapoor's Eetha faced a title row as the NCP and Vithabai Narayangaonkar's family demand the legendary Marathi folk artiste's name be included.

Kunal Kemmu dismisses Alliance-Lock Upp rivalry: No competition with Farah, Riteish Actor Kunal Kemmu said he does not see Alliance and Lock Upp: Sach ya Sazaa as rivals. He said audiences would choose what to watch as he reflected on the appeal and ethics of reality television.

Jr NTR, Trivikram Srinivas reunite for a grand mythological drama on Lord Muruga Jr NTR and Trivikram Srinivas have officially reunited for an ambitious mythological drama, ending months of speculation. The film will be backed by NTR Arts and Haarika & Hassine Creations.

Internet questions Ram Kapoor's conduct after he blocks Lock Upp 2 task over water Ram Kapoor has landed in fresh controversy after the latest episode of Lock Upp 2. Here's what happened during the task that sparked a wave of criticism online.

Voicemails for Isabelle breaks your heart before putting it back together Director Leah McKendrick's Voicemails for Isabelle follows Jill and Wes as their romance grows through grief. The film turns sisterhood, healing and chance into a warm love story with real emotional weight.

Will SS Rajamouli's Varanasi have more parts? Director shares update Filmmaker SS Rajamouli said that his upcoming drama Varanasi is being made as a single film. He said its spectacle across Antarctica and Ramayana-inspired fantasy is anchored in a father-son bond.

Priyadarshan exits Hera Pheri 3, says it may never hit the screen Priyadarshan has confirmed he is not associated with Hera Pheri 3 at present. His exit adds to the film's unresolved legal disputes and uncertainty over its release.

K Bhagyaraj's son Shanthnu, daughter Sharanya pay emotional tribute to late father Shanthnu Bhagyaraj and Sharanya Bhagyaraj shared emotional memories of their father, K Bhagyaraj, days after his death. Their posts drew condolences and underscored the filmmaker's enduring bond with family and Tamil cinema.

Suriya reunites with TJ Gnanavel as Hombale Films announces new project Hombale Films announced its maiden collaboration with Suriya. Directed by TJ Gnanavel, the yet-untitled film stars Kayadu Lohar and features music by Sai Abhyankkar.

Tom Holland hints at Miles Morales' Marvel debut ahead of Brand New Day release Actor Tom Holland said he wants Miles Morales to enter the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The remarks have renewed focus on the character's live-action future before Spider-Man: Brand New Day.

general news.headline: Chinese encroachment in Arunachal? Indian Army calls reports incorrectThe Indian Army has denied reports that the Chinese PLA set up camps inside Arunachal Pradesh. The rebuttal came after a local group's letter alleged fresh occupation at five sites near Taksing.

Kerala CM writes to Bengal CM over passport denial to veteran journalistV D Satheesan has written to Suvendu Adhikari seeking urgent intervention in R Rajagopal's passport renewal case. The move came after an adverse Kolkata Police verification reportedly linked the delay to his electoral roll exclusion.

Not our lawyer, threatening us: Siya Goyal's brother adds twist to murder probeSpeaking exclusively to India Today, Sahil said neither he nor his family had authorised lawyer Aashutosh Srivastava to represent them in the case. He further alleged that Srivastava may have obtained Siya's signature "by deceit", though he did not elaborate on the circumstances.

BRA 0 - 1 JPN Live Scores and Update: Sano gives Japan lead over Brazil in HoustonBrazil (BRA) vs Japan (JPN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live Scores and Update: (BRA 0-1 JPN) Despite dominating possession, Brazil have conceded first in Houston as as long range by Sano goes through Alisson's reach.

Pakistan's Khuda Hafiz, Hello Harappa is a copycat moveFor 70 years, Pakistan looked to Arabia for its origins. Now it is looking beneath its own feet. What changed, and why is Islamabad suddenly reaching for a past it once called mushrik?

Ayodhya Bar asks Champat Rai to leave town, won't defend Ram temple theft accusedThe Faizabad Bar Association has asked Champat Rai, Anil Mishra and Gopal Rao to leave Ayodhya within three days. The resolution came amid anger over alleged embezzlement of devotees' donations and included a boycott of the accused.

Gold prices fall: Indians sell old jewellery as fears of deeper correction growAs gold prices retreat from record highs, Indian households are selling old jewellery to lock in gains. The trend is lifting organised recycling volumes and may help reduce reliance on imports.

Punish even the powerful: CM Vijay to officials, promises no political interferenceTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay directed district collectors and police chiefs to take fearless, impartial action against criminals without political interference, stressing that even the most powerful offenders must be punished under the law.
Why AC explosions are becoming more common in Delhi-NCRA Noida AC blast has raised safety concerns as the region faces extreme heat. Here's why air conditioners explode, and how to prevent them.

Blatant assault on sovereignty: India shreds Pak over air strikes on AfghanistanIn a sharply worded statement, the Ministry of External Affairs accused Pakistan of carrying out an "act of aggression" that not only undermined Afghanistan's territorial integrity but also threatened peace and stability across the region.

Severe heatwave grips Europe; over 1,300 deaths, wildfires rage across continentScientists have described the ongoing heatwave as the worst ever in Europe, with extreme temperatures straining healthcare systems, disrupting power generation and damaging critical infrastructure across several countries. Forecasters, however, expect cooler conditions to return to much of Western Europe later this week.

Day 1 LIVE: Aryna Sabalenka in action, Jannik Sinner into Round 2Wimbledon 2026, Day 1 Live Updates: Jannik Sinner has beaten Miomir Kecmanovic in five sets on Centre Court and advanced to the second round. Aryna Sabalenka is in action against Teodora Kostovic. Later, Novak Djokovic will be in action.

Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan faces backlash from Rajput group over title, first glimpseAjay Devgn's film Chauhaan has drawn objections from the Kshatriya Parishad over its title and first glimpse. The group says Rajput history and identity should not be used for contemporary political narratives.

Delhi's EV push: E-cars under Rs 30 lakh tax-free, no new petrol bikes from 2028In a major push to tackle air pollution that routinely chokes Delhi, the Cabinet approved a new EV Policy that exempts electric cars priced up to Rs 30 lakh from road tax and registration fees and charts a phased transition to cleaner transport from 2028.

Heavy rains to dominate Northeast, Bihar on Tuesday, heatwave in Delhi-NCRDespite the advancing monsoon, heatwave conditions are expected to persist in isolated parts of Delhi, Haryana and Chandigarh on June 30, but are expected to ease by early July.

Women Air Force officers get relief as Delhi High Court pauses their dischargeWomen Short Service Commission officers of the Indian Air Force have moved the Delhi High Court against the denial of Permanent Commission and their release orders. They say the release violated the Supreme Court's interim protection while their legal remedies remained pending.

Who was Sarla Bhat? Why her murder became a landmark Kashmir terror caseSarla Bhat's kidnap and murder case has become a symbol of the long and difficult search for justice in terror crimes linked to the insurgency in Kashmir.

6 killed in shooting at Germany youth facility, suspect detainedThe shooting occurred in Stade, a town of nearly 50,000 people west of Hamburg. Authorities said the circumstances surrounding the incident remain under investigation.

Govt to lift petrol and diesel sales curbs from July 1 as supply concerns easeThe precautionary measures were introduced to protect domestic fuel supplies at a time when uncertainty in the global energy market was increasing.

Ram Temple donation theft accused built Rs 25-lakh house on Rs 12,000 salaryRam Mandir donation theft accused Lavkush Mishra, a cash-counting staffer earning around Rs 12,000 a month, acquired land and built a house now worth around Rs 25 lakh after joining the temple trust.

Biker cheats death after Arunachal landslide almost sweeps him off mountain roadA biker was caught in a landslide while crossing a damaged, mud-covered road in Arunachal Pradesh. The video has highlighted how relentless rain is making travel dangerous across north-east India.

Death penalty for man, 65, for 3-year-old's rape-murder; court says rarest of rareA 65-year-old man was given a death penalty by a Pune court for luring a 3-year-old girl to a cattle shed in Nasrapur village and then raping and murdering her on May 1, 2026.

Delhi man receives less salary, confronts HR on camera. Their reaction is enragingA Delhi-based telecom company employee confronted the HR team in a viral office video over an alleged salary shortfall. The exchange has sparked wider debate online.

CBSE's 3-language policy just got clearer. Here are the 7 biggest updatesCBSE has issued a detailed clarification on its three-language policy, answering key questions on transition rules, board exams, foreign languages, textbooks and exemptions for current and future student batches.

sports.headline: Jannik Sinner explains playing with blood on shoe after nasty fall at Wimbledon Jannik Sinner brushed off concerns after playing through a bleeding toe to stage a remarkable five-set comeback against Miomir Kecmanovic in the Wimbledon 2026.

Saudi football's big ambitions hit wall as FA president Al-Misehal resigns after WC exit FIFA World Cup 2026: Saudi Arabia's World Cup exit has cost Yasser Al-Misehal his job after seven years in charge. The resignation exposes fresh questions over a lavish football project still short on tournament substance.

Jannik Sinner survives huge 5-set scare, battles injury to reach Round 2 Jannik Sinner battled through a gruelling 3-hour-30-minute five-set win over Miomir Kecmanovic at Wimbledon 2026, overcoming an injury scare and a tiebreak setback to advance to Round 2.

BRA 0 - 1 JPN Live Scores and Update: Japan leads Brazil in Houston Brazil (BRA) vs Japan (JPN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live Scores and Update: (BRA 0-1 JPN) Despite dominating possession, Brazil have conceded first in Houston as as long range by Sano goes through Alisson's reach.

No Alcaraz, no free pass: Vijay Amritraj sees tough Wimbledon for Jannik Sinner Despite Carlos Alcaraz's injury-enforced absence, Vijay Amritraj believes defending champion Jannik Sinner will face a stern test in his bid to retain the Wimbledon title, insisting there are no free passes at the All England Club.

How Australia flipped the script to send India crashing out: Ash Gardner reveals Women's T20 World Cup: Ash Gardner revealed Australia overcame the pressure of a steep chase by targeting favourable match-ups, breaking the innings into smaller phases and backing their batting depth to attack India's bowlers.

Maja Chwalinska goes from French Open fairytale to Wimbledon heartbreak Maja Chwalinska's Wimbledon campaign ended in heartbreaking fashion after the Polish star squandered a set, a 5-2 lead and a match point before losing to Mananchaya Sawangkaew in the first round.

India vs Pakistan unlikely at 2028 Olympics. Cricket qualification rules explained Cricket's LA28 qualification rules have placed India firmly on track for Los Angeles while pushing Pakistan towards a treacherous global qualifier. By heavily prioritising regional rankings, the newly unveiled roadmap drastically raises the stakes for every bilateral series—leaving a blockbuster India-Pakistan Olympic clash far from guaranteed.

Day 1 LIVE: Aryna Sabalenka in action, Jannik Sinner into Round 2 Wimbledon 2026, Day 1 Live Updates: Jannik Sinner has beaten Miomir Kecmanovic in five sets on Centre Court and advanced to the second round. Aryna Sabalenka is in action against Teodora Kostovic. Later, Novak Djokovic will be in action.

Iceland Cricket trolls Gambhir after India's historic series loss vs Ireland India's loss to Ireland triggered anger amongst cricket fans. The Gautam Gambhir-coached team faltered against the European side for the first time in the history of the game.

India women's cricket team qualifies for Olympics despite early World Cup exit India Women qualified for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics despite their group-stage exit from the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, earning Asia's lone automatic berth under the ICC's newly announced qualification pathway.

Ireland coach Heinrich Malan steps down a day after historic India series winHeinrich Malan stepped down as Ireland men's coach a day after a 2-0 T20I series sweep over India in Belfast. The move gives Cricket Ireland time to prepare for the next 50-over World Cup cycle.

After 3-day search through earthquake rubble, footballer finds family deadArgentine footballer Lucas Trejo spent three days searching through the rubble of his family home in Venezuela after last week's earthquakes. Authorities later recovered the bodies of his wife and two children.

I am there: Srikanth confident of brighter days after US Open runner-up finishKidambi Srikanth finished runner-up at the US Open after a hard-fought three-game final against Su Li-yang. The run strengthened his belief that his game is close to breaking through again.

Brazil vs Japan: FIFA World Cup prepares for potential classicBrazil return to action in the FIFA World Cup, as the 5-time champions take on Asia's best football team Japan. Will Brazil's Samba outmatch the underdogs, or will the Samurai cut down the Brazilian flair?

Was Ben Stokes forced to retire? England captain breaks silence on selfish callEngland captain Ben Stokes dropped a mid-match retirement bombshell at Trent Bridge, telling teammates the reasons could wait, before opening up on burnout and the nightclub controversy that clouded his final weeks.

Wimbledon: Emma Raducanu withdraws, fit-again Sinner ready to battle heatEmma Raducanu has withdrawn from Wimbledon after scans confirmed a stress fracture in her lower right leg. Jannik Sinner, meanwhile, says rising temperatures are reshaping preparations for his title defence.

Sooryavanshi should wait: India remain stubborn ahead of England T20I seriesVaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for international cricket but needs to bide his time, said India assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate after the team's historic series loss against Ireland. Does this mean that Vaibhav will not play in England as well?

Wimbledon Day 1: Sinner, Djokovic start as London heat easesWimbledon 2026 opens with Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic and Aryna Sabalenka leading Day 1 on Centre Court. Their starts come as London's heat eases, though changed conditions remain a factor.

Days after Jaspal Rana's death, shooter's mother dies of cardiac arrestLess than three weeks after Indian shooting legend Jaspal Rana's death, his family suffered another tragedy as his mother Shyama Devi passed away following prolonged health complications and hospitalisation.

Cape Verde captain under investigation for alleged rape ahead of Argentina clashCape Verde's historic FIFA World Cup run has been overshadowed by serious allegations. Brazilian publication Globo is reporting that the team's captain, Ryan Mendes, is under investigation in New Zealand over an alleged sexual assault during the FIFA Series in March.

India women self-destruct, have no one else to blame for T20 World Cup exitWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India crashed out of the tournament after a six-wicket defeat to Australia at Lord's on Sunday. The loss highlighted familiar shortcomings in bowling, fielding and decision-making, exposing the areas India must address if they are to consistently challenge the world's best.

Delayed or denied? Sooryavanshi snub splits opinion after Ireland shockerVaibhav Sooryavanshi was benched throughout India's series defeat to Ireland, splitting opinion among former players. Gavaskar, Kaif and Tiwary back his inclusion. Ashwin backs the management. The debate now shadows India's England tour from July 1.

Canada end brave Bafana Bafana dream with stoppage-time heartbreakStephen Eustaquio scored deep in stoppage time as Canada beat South Africa 1-0 in Los Angeles. The defeat ended Bafana Bafana's brave World Cup run, while Canada reached the Round of 16 for the first time.

Serena Williams reveals why she accepted Wimbledon singles comebackSerena Williams admits she nearly walked away from one final Wimbledon singles opportunity before convincing herself to embrace the moment. The 23-time Grand Slam champion will return to Centre Court after almost four years away, insisting she could not let the chance slip by.

Lack joy and happiness: Quaresma explains Ronaldo-led Portugal's strugglesFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal scraped into the knockout stage after a flat goalless draw with Colombia. The stale display has intensified scrutiny of Cristiano Ronaldo, midfield control and the side's wider cohesion issues.

South Korea coach resigns after World Cup exit as President Lee lashes outFIFA World Cup 2026: Hong Myung-bo resigned after South Korea crashed out in the World Cup group stage. The exit provoked President Lee's attack on football administration and renewed scrutiny of the appointment process.

Harmanpreet to continue as India captain? India coach Amol Muzumdar opens upWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: India head coach Amol Muzumdar addressed questions over Harmanpreet Kaur's future as captain following India's early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup.

One last wicket, one final swing: Ben Stokes gives Trent Bridge a goodbye to rememberENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes' final innings for England lasted just 20 balls, but it was filled with everything that defined his career. After announcing his shock retirement, the England captain signed off with a fearless 30, a standing ovation and a guard of honour from New Zealand.

RSA 0-1 CAN Highlights: Canada advance to Round of 16 with late winSouth Africa (RSA) vs Canada (CAN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: Canada left it painfully late, but Stephen Eustaquio's stoppage-time strike finally broke South African resistance to send Jesse Marsch's side into the Round of 16 with a 1-0 win over South Africa in LA.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
